reasoning: While the fidelity score is high, the programmatic shingling recall (0.636) and the specific findings indicate a significant content loss of approximately 40%. The omission of body text for Sections 6 and 8 (committee reports) is a substantial failure that undermines the document's utility, despite the LLM sub-system mis-categorizing these defects as minor.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There is a major failure in capturing the descriptive paragraphs within Section 6 and Section 8. The output lists the sub-section headings but entirely omits the body text following them. This includes the entirety of the reports for the Local Government Committee, State Government Users Group, Federal Interagency Committee (on Page 2), the Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee, Technical Advisory Committee, LiDAR Data, and Future M&O Meetings (on Page 3). Roughly 40% of the document's total text
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Multiple missing text blocks including: 'The LGC last met on November 20th...'; 'The executive committee meeting was last week...'; 'There are two new members...'; 'Paul mentioned that the year 2022...'; 'Dan is stepping down...'; 'Hope reported that Esri...'; 'March 16, June 22...'
concerns.output_quote: The output for these sections contains only list items with the titles: 'Local Government Committee – Debbie Brannan', 'State Government Users Group Committee – John Farley', 'Federal Interagency Committee – Scott Lokken', 'd. Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee – Paul Badr', 'e. Technical Advisory Committee – Dan Madding', 'a. LiDAR Data', 'b. Future M&O Meetings'.
concerns.location: Sections 6 and 8, Pages 2 and 3, Element indices 24, 26, and 30.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Major sections 1 through 5 are treated as list items within individual list objects (elements 9, 11, 13, 15, 19), whereas major sections 6 through 9 are promoted to Heading Level 2 (elements 23, 27, 29, 31). This creates a fragmented and inconsistent navigational structure compared to the source where these sections are functionally equivalent.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1. Welcome, 2. Approval of Minutes..., 3. NC Parcels..., 4. Census..., 5. Statewide Orthoimagery..., 6. Status Reports..., 7. February 12, 2020..., 8. Other Items, 9. Adjourn
concerns.output_quote: Reading order 9, 11, 13, 15, 19 (lists) vs Reading order 23, 27, 29, 31 (H2 headings)

concerns.analysis: The structural reading order is fundamentally broken due to the total omission of body content for sub-sections. Specifically, the descriptive paragraphs for sections 6a through 6e (Local Government, State Government, Federal Interagency, Statewide Mapping, and Technical Advisory committees) and sections 8a and 8b (LiDAR Data and Future Meetings) are entirely missing. This results in an illogical flow where headings are provided without the information they are intended to introduce.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Paragraphs beginning with: 'The LGC last met on November 20th...', 'The executive committee meeting...', 'There are two new members...', 'Paul mentioned that the year 2022...', 'Dan is stepping down...', 'Hope reported that Esri...', and 'March 16, June 22...'
concerns.output_quote: [The JSON contains list items for these sections at reading_order 24, 26, and 30, but no paragraph elements follow them.]

concerns.analysis: The primary numbered list from the source (items 1-5) has been fragmented into five separate, single-item unordered lists. This breaks the structural relationship between these items. Additionally, the content belonging to the sub-lists in section 6 and 8 is missing as noted in S4.

action_items: Re-extract or manually insert the missing body text for the Local Government Committee, State Government Users Group, Federal Interagency Committee, SMAC, TAC, and LiDAR data sections.
Unify the heading hierarchy so that sections 1-9 use consistent Heading Level tags.
Re-group the fragmented list items into a single coherent list structure.
